messages.content:
Assignment
You are working for a medium sized IT services company developing products and
providing services for the clients which is expected to contribute to the growth of the
company.


DevSphere Services Inc.
DevSphere Services Inc. is a medium sized multi-national company having its
development centres in USA and Nepal. The administrative departments: Finance,
Human Resources Management, and Marketing are based at the Corporate Office in
Florida, USA.

Nepal is the Development centre and has its local president along with a pool of
Managers and other required Development Team members.

Board
Kevin Brown Chief Executive Officer
Robert Smith Chief Operating Officer
Brad Kasper Chief Financial Officer
Julia Stephenson Chief HR officer
Richard Wilson Chief Technology Officer
Howles Buffet President – USA
Kedar Basnet President - Nepal
Kyle Jones Director of Project Management
Helen Peterson Director of Sales and Marketing


Each country, region has their own management, engineering team. The company
works on multiple projects as per the requirements from different clients. The company
has just signed a contract with a new client (a Multiplex company) to create a software
application for them - “Show Time”, a mobile app that allows users for easy booking of
the movie tickets.


Functional Requirements for “Show Time” app

This app has several features to provide users with booking the movie ticket
experience, and provide the admin the control over the data. Features of the app is
listed out below:

User Panel:
● Registration and Log-in: User can provide details such as name, email, phone
number and password, a user can register themselves on the app. Using the
credentials, a user can log-in within the app.
● Show Listings: User can optimally showcase a list of shows right from show
category to show name, venue & location, offers, ratings, as well as booking
charges. Users should be given with the ability to search something they are
looking perhaps with a type-ahead style search box.
● Show Detail: Right after selecting a desired movie or event, the user gets
directed to further details such as event/show description like name, images, and
duration and later with data like different venues with date and time, customer
satisfaction ratings and reviews, and more information.
● Show Booking: Provide Book Ticket icon as a feature that re-direct users to a
particular cinema, show list, show timings, graphical representation of seat
arrangements to select the desired number of seat easily and then book.
● Booking Screen: Users can view the order summary, which may consist of: Show
Name; Image, Date; Time, Venue Name, Ticket Price, Seat Number, Total Price,
View and confirm Email ID/Phone Number.
● Payment Options: Through this feature, users can make payments seamlessly
through a number of channels.
● Reviews and Rating: User can rate, review and share their feedback.


Admin Panel:
● Data Control: With the help of a Dashboard, an admin can look and control over
all the data that is taking place within the application. Be it user list, active users,
add new users or may edit/remove users. This panel also comprises all the
details and number of listed cinemas, movies, total booking, total earning, and so
much more. From this place, an admin can also manage shows and venues.


Non-Functional Requirements
● The platform’s website should handle 100,000 users at a time without affecting its
performance
● Should support on iOS and Android platform
● Maximum 5 seconds of response time
● Disaster recovery


You need to carry out this project, for which you are required to perform the tasks which will be listed below

1. Project Initiation:
   a. Conduct a kick-off meeting to introduce the project team and stakeholders
   b. Define the roles and responsibilities of each team member
   c. Create a Project Charter and get it approved by the stakeholders

2. Project Planning:
   a. Develop a detailed project plan outlining tasks, timelines, and resources
   b. Create a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) to break down tasks into manageable components
   c. Develop a risk management plan to address potential risks and mitigation strategies
   d. Develop a communication plan for the project team and stakeholders
   e. Determine project milestones and deliverables

3. Design and Development:
   a. Finalize the app's UI/UX design elements, including user flow and wireframes
   b. Create system architecture and database design for the application
   c. Develop the app for iOS and Android platforms, ensuring compliance with non-functional requirements
   d. Implement payment gateways and other third-party integrations
   e. Create an admin panel for data control and management

4. Testing:
   a. Develop a test plan and test cases for the application
   b. Conduct functionality, usability, performance, and security testing
   c. Address any bugs or issues identified during testing
   d. Repeat testing and bug fixing until the application meets the requirements

5. Deployment:
   a. Prepare the app for deployment on the respective app stores
   b. Conduct necessary pre-launch activities, such as setting up app store accounts and provisioning profiles
   c. Deploy the app to the iOS App Store and Google Play Store
   d. Set up and configure the disaster recovery plan

6. Training and Support:
   a. Train the client's team on how to use the app and admin panel
   b. Provide documentation and user guides for the app and its features
   c. Offer ongoing support and maintenance services to address issues, improvements or updates

7. Project Closure:
   a. Conduct a project review to analyze the project performance
   b. Identify lessons learned and opportunities for improvement for future projects
   c. Celebrate the project success with the team and stakeholders
   d. Hand over the final deliverables and necessary documentation to the client
   e. Close the project and release resources for other projects

Consider the various approaches and system/software development methods
that might be appropriate for this particular firm and developing this particular
system. Explain in outline two substantially different methods – and compare
their suitability for this case. Make recommendations in relation to this specific
case – giving a full rational.

Approach 1: Agile Methodology (Scrum)

Scrum is an Agile framework that allows flexibility and fast-paced development. In Scrum, the project is divided into small iterations called sprints, usually lasting 2-4 weeks. A specific set of tasks are planned and carried out in each sprint. Scrum emphasizes close collaboration among the team, stakeholders, and the client.

Pros:
- Regular delivery of incremental features ensures continuous feedback from the client and allows adaptation to changing requirements.
- Faster time-to-market and increased customer satisfaction due to iterative and incremental development.
- Improved team collaboration and communication due to daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and sprint retrospectives.
- Enhanced risk management through continuous monitoring and inspecting project progress during each sprint.

Cons:
- Requires well-trained, dedicated, and self-managing teams that may not always be available.
- Prioritization of tasks is important and can be difficult when working on complex projects.

Approach 2: Waterfall Methodology

The Waterfall methodology is a linear and sequential software development method where each phase of the project is completed before moving on to the next one. It typically consists of several phases such as requirements, design, development, testing, deployment, and maintenance.

Pros:
- Easy to understand and follow, especially for organizations accustomed to this method.
- Clearly defined stages and requirements ensure that all expected features are documented and planned upfront, reducing the likelihood of scope creep.
- Allows for potential early identification of issues during the design stage, lowering the risk of major problems in development.
- Progress can be easily measured as each phase is completed.

Cons:
- Less flexible to changes, as any modification to the scope can lead to extensive delays and increased costs.
- Full functionality and performance of the system are only available after the entire project is completed, leading to longer time-to-market.
- Does not encourage continuous feedback from the client, which may affect their satisfaction.

Recommendation:

For the "Show Time" app project, Agile Methodology (Scrum) would be more suitable due to the following reasons:
1. The app's requirements may change during development as new technologies emerge or as the client refines their needs. Agile can accommodate these changes without disrupting the entire project.
2. The iterative nature of Scrum allows for faster delivery of features, giving the client early visibility of the application, and the opportunity for feedback that can be incorporated into the development process.
3. The Scrum framework fosters better collaboration among teams and stakeholders, increasing the overall efficiency and quality of the end product.
4. The Agile approach allows better risk management by often reviewing the progress and adapting accordingly in each sprint, mitigating potential issues early on.

In conclusion, Agile Methodology (Scrum) offers greater flexibility, adaptability, and a more client-centric approach, making it better suited for developing the "Show Time" app when compared to Waterfall Methodology.

In line with the Prince2 methodology, produce a RACI Matrix chart giving a clear
insight into setup of your project team, the chart should clearly identify the
champions and their roles in the project. Useful templates can be found on the
Official PRINCE2 website. (Note: Keeping in mind the case study, you are free to
make assumptions when setting up your team members and their role).

A RACI Matrix chart helps in understanding the roles and responsibilities of team members in a project using the following identifiers: Responsible (R), Accountable (A), Consulted (C), and Informed (I).

Below is an example of a RACI Matrix chart for the "Show Time" app project based on the PRINCE2 methodology:

| Tasks/Activities        | Project Manager (PM) | App Developer (AD) | UI/UX Designer (UX) | QA Engineer (QA) | Client (CL) | Marketing Team (MT) |
|-------------------------|------|----|----|----|----|----|
| Project Initiation      | A    |    |    |    | C  |    |
| Project Plan Creation   | A/R  |    |    |    | C  |    |
| Requirement Gathering   | A/R  | C  | C  | C  | C  | C  |
| UI/UX Design            | R    |    | A  |    | C  |    |
| App Development         | R    | A  |    |    | C  |    |
| Database Design         | R    | A  |    |    | C  |    |
| Integration & Testing   | R    | R  | R  | A  | I  |    |
| Deployment              | A/R  | R  |    | R  | C  |    |
| User Training           | R    | C  | C  |    | A  |    |
| App Marketing           | R    |    |    |    | C  | A  |
| Project Closure         | A    | R  | R  | R  | C  | R  |

Key Role Descriptions:

- Project Manager (PM): Overall responsibility for project initiation, planning, execution, monitoring, and closure. Manages communication with stakeholders, including the client.
- App Developer (AD): Responsible for building the app, incorporating changes based on feedback, and ensuring compliance with non-functional requirements.
- UI/UX Designer (UX): Responsible for designing the user interface and user experience of the app, including wireframes and prototypes, and ensuring that the app adheres to design guidelines.
- QA Engineer (QA): Responsible for testing the app, identifying bugs or defects, and ensuring that the app meets the defined quality standards.
- Client (CL): Provides input on project requirements, gives feedback during development, and ultimately approves deliverables.
- Marketing Team (MT): Responsible for generating awareness, promoting the app, and driving user acquisition once the app is launched.

Produce an outline plan for the development on the basis of your chosen system
development method or methodology with justification of plan following the
selected methodology.

As recommended earlier, we have chosen the Agile Methodology (Scrum) for developing the "Show Time" app. Below is an outline plan for the development following the Scrum framework:

1. Project Initiation:
   - Obtain project approval and sign-off from stakeholders.
   - Conduct a kick-off meeting to introduce project goals and objectives, and to confirm the team members' roles and responsibilities.

2. Create Product Backlog:
   - Gather and document requirements in consultation with the client and other stakeholders.
   - Develop a prioritized Product Backlog containing user stories, features, and enhancements required for the app.

3. Define Sprints:
   - Break down the project into a series of time-boxed sprints, usually 2-4 weeks long, with specific goals and deliverables identified for each sprint.

4. Sprint Planning:
   - For each sprint:
      a. Select a set of high-priority items from the Product Backlog to be completed in the sprint.
      b. Develop a detailed plan outlining tasks, resources, and timelines for the selected items.

5. Daily Stand-ups:
   - Organize daily meetings with team members to discuss progress, roadblocks, and plan for the day.

6. Sprint Execution:
   - Team members work on their respective tasks to complete the sprint goals, collaborating with each other and the client as necessary to ensure that the requirements are met.

7. Sprint Review:
   - At the end of each sprint, review the completed work with the client and stakeholders and gather feedback.
   - Update the Product Backlog with new items, changes, or reprioritized tasks based on the feedback.

8. Sprint Retrospective:
   - Conduct a meeting at the end of each sprint to review the team's performance, identify areas of improvement, and implement the necessary changes in the next sprint.

9. Repeat steps 4-8 for subsequent sprints until the Product Backlog is complete or the project goals have been met.

10. App Deployment and Launch:
   - Prepare the app for deployment on the iOS App Store and Google Play Store.
   - Conduct necessary pre-launch activities and deploy the app to the respective stores.

11. Project Closure:
   - Review the overall project performance, including scope, requirements, budget, and timeline.
   - Conduct a retrospective meeting to identify lessons learned and opportunities for improvement in future projects.
   - Close the project and transition the necessary documentation, data, and support responsibilities to the client or relevant parties.

The Agile (Scrum) methodology is chosen because it allows for flexibility, faster delivery of features, and continuous feedback from the client, ensuring better quality of the end product. This methodology encourages adaptation to changes and accommodates evolving requirements, making it more suitable for the dynamic nature of mobile app development. Regular client involvement and feedback throughout the project also increase the chances of delivering a product that aligns with the client's expectations and needs.
also provide justification

Justification for choosing Agile Methodology (Scrum):

1. Flexibility and adaptability: The Scrum framework allows for faster and easier incorporation of changes in project requirements. This is particularly valuable in the field of mobile app development, where technologies and market dynamics can change rapidly. The Agile approach enables the team to adapt to new requirements and pivots without wasting resources or expanding timelines significantly.

2. Incremental and iterative development: Agile, with its iterative development approach, offers continuous delivery of incremental features, allowing the client to evaluate progress and provide feedback regularly. This ensures that the development process stays aligned with the client's expectations and minimizes the risk of rework or incorrect implementations. By focusing on smaller increments, the team can quickly identify and address any issues or improvements, ensuring an overall higher-quality product.

3. Faster time-to-market: With Scrum, completed features can be released more frequently than in other methodologies, allowing the client to benefit from an earlier return on investment. Moreover, short feedback loops enable the client to introduce the app to the market with initial key features, and subsequent enhancements in response to market feedback further improve the product.

4. Improved risk management and visibility: The Scrum methodology involves regular inspections of project progress through sprint reviews, providing a better understanding of project status and highlighting any potential risks, issues, or changes. This enables the project team to proactively mitigate risks and make necessary adjustments in a timely manner. In addition, stakeholders can monitor progress more closely and maintain transparency throughout the project lifecycle.

5. Enhanced collaboration and communication: Scrum fosters better communication and collaboration among team members and stakeholders due to its framework that involves daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives. The emphasis on teamwork and continuous improvement promotes a positive work environment with shared goals, resulting in higher productivity and efficiency.

In summary, the Agile Methodology (Scrum) is justified for the "Show Time" app project due to its ability to adapt to changing requirements, deliver high-quality software, and maintain strong communication and collaboration. Its emphasis on incremental, iterative development ensures faster time-to-market and continuous improvement of the product, ultimately leading to higher client satisfaction and better overall outcomes for the project.

Using relevant PRINCE2 templates produce a Project Brief which provides a
foundation for the initiation of the project. Useful templates can be found via the
Official PRINCE2 website.

Title: Show Time App Development

Project Definition (Purpose, Objectives, and Scope):

Purpose: To develop a mobile app named "Show Time" to simplify movie ticket bookings for our Multiplex client, offering both customers and Multiplex administrators an intuitive and smooth experience for booking and managing movie tickets.

Objective:
1. Design and develop a user-friendly "Show Time" app for both iOS and Android platforms.
2. Include functionalities for user registration and login, show listings and details, ticket booking, payments, reviews and ratings, and an admin panel for data control.
3. Achieve a response time of a maximum of 5 seconds and support 100,000 concurrent users without affecting the app's performance.
4. Prepare and execute a disaster recovery plan.

Scope:
1. Development of User Panel features including Registration, Login, Show Listings, Show Details, Show Booking, Booking Screen, Payment Options, and Reviews and Ratings.
2. Development of Admin Panel for Data Control.
3. The App should support iOS and Android platforms.

Project Deliverables:
1. The completed "Show Time" app for iOS and Android platforms.
2. App deployment on the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.
3. User guides and technical documentation.
4. Training and support for the client.

Constraints and Assumptions:

Constraints:
1. Budget limits for the project.
2. Deadline to complete the project within the agreed timeline.
3. Ensuring app compatibility with different iOS and Android devices.

Assumptions:
1. Availability of all required resources, such as skilled team members, tools, and technology.
2. Coordination and cooperation from the client in providing necessary inputs and approvals in a timely manner.
3. Reliable third-party services for integration (payment gateways).

Project Approach:
Agile Methodology (Scrum) will be followed for the development of the "Show Time" app. This approach allows flexibility, adaptability, faster feature delivery, and continuous feedback from the client.

Project Management Team Structure:
1. Project Manager
2. App Developers
3. UI/UX Designers
4. QA Engineers
5. Marketing Team

Outline Business Case:
The development of the "Show Time" app will provide our client with an advanced platform to simplify movie ticket bookings, thereby attracting more customers and increasing revenues. The app will enhance the overall customer experience, leading to a larger customer base, higher user retention, and improved brand image. The ROI is expected to be realized within a year of the app's launch, with continuous growth in the target market.

Project Timelines:
The estimated timeline for the "Show Time" app development project is 4-6 months, including design, development, testing, deployment, and post-launch support.

Overall Project Risk:
Potential risks include technical issues, limited resources, changing client requirements, and third-party integrations. These risks will be managed using a continuous risk assessment process and agile methodologies to ensure project success.

Outline the subsequent stages the work will go through – taking into account the
requirements of PRINCE2 and your chosen System/Software Development
method – and relating these to the specific case study scenario.

Incorporating PRINCE2 methodology with the Agile Methodology (Scrum), here are the subsequent stages the "Show Time" app project will go through, with steps tailored to the specific case study:

1. Starting up a Project (SU):
   - Appoint the project sponsor and project manager.
   - Create a Project Brief (as outlined in the previous answer) and a high-level project plan.
   - Define the Agile (Scrum) team structure, including roles and responsibilities.

2. Initiating a Project (IP):
   - Develop a detailed Project Initiation Document (PID) incorporating project objectives, scope, deliverables, timeline, cost, and team roles.
   - Define the Agile process, including sprint duration, sprint planning, sprint execution, and sprint review.
   - Establish the project quality, risk, and communication management plans.

3. Directing a Project (DP):
   - Organize steering committee meetings to review project progress, address any issues, and approve major decisions.
   - Monitor project value, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and client expectations.
   - Facilitate necessary resources and support for the project team.

4. Controlling a Stage (CS):
   - Implement the Agile (Scrum) methodology during project execution:
      a. Develop a prioritized product backlog.
      b. Schedule and conduct sprint planning meetings.
      c. Conduct daily stand-ups to monitor progress and address concerns.
      d. Track sprint progress based on the defined sprint goals and project performance metrics.
      e. Facilitate sprint reviews and retrospectives for continuous improvement.
   - Manage any identified risks, issues, or changes within the project.

5. Managing Product Delivery (MP):
   - Oversee product iteration and feature development, maintaining focus on the client's requirements.
   - Ensure that the project maintains its quality standards through rigorous testing and client validation.
   - Coordinate with the client and stakeholders to gather feedback and integrate it into the development process.

6. Managing Stage Boundaries (SB):
   - Review project progress and performance at the end of each sprint, and make any necessary adjustments for the next sprint.
   - Update the product backlog based on new requirements, client feedback, or reprioritized tasks.
   - Keep the steering committee informed about project status, risks, and issues.

7. Closing a Project (CP):
   - Ensure that all project deliverables have been completed and are approved by the client.
   - Conduct a final project review and retrospective to identify lessons learned and areas for improvement in future projects.
   - Archive project documents and hand over the app, support, and all necessary materials to the client.
   - Close out the project, releasing resources and appreciating the team's efforts.

By following the PRINCE2 methodology, integrated with Agile (Scrum), the "Show Time" app project can successfully balance structure, flexibility, and adaptability. This approach will facilitate effective project management, efficient development, and high-quality deliverables, ultimately benefiting the client and contributing to the growth of the IT services company.

The stages of Agile should be mapped with the processes of PRINCE 2 side to side with respect to
the given case-study in table form

The following table maps the stages of Agile (Scrum) with the processes of PRINCE2 in the context of the "Show Time" app project:

| Agile (Scrum) Stages      | PRINCE2 Processes        | Project Activities in Context of "Show Time" App |
|---------------------------|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| Project Kick-off          | Starting up a Project (SU)| - Appoint project sponsor and manager            |
|                           |                          | - Create Project Brief and high-level plan       |
|                           |                          | - Define Scrum team structure                     |
|                           | Initiating a Project (IP)| - Develop Project Initiation Document (PID)      |
|                           |                          | - Establish project management plans             |
| Sprint Planning           | Directing a Project (DP) |  - Schedule and conduct sprint planning meetings |
|                           | Controlling a Stage (CS) |  - Monitor sprint progress and address issues    |
| Sprint Execution          | Managing Product Delivery (MP)|- Develop features for "Show Time" app         |
| Daily Stand-ups           | Controlling a Stage (CS) |  - Conduct daily stand-ups to discuss progress   |
|                           |                          |  - Track progress based on sprint goals           |
| Sprint Review             | Managing Stage Boundaries (SB)| - Review progress at end of each sprint       |
|                           |                          |  - Update product backlog based on feedback      |
| Sprint Retrospective      | Controlling a Stage (CS) | - Conduct retrospective for continuous improvement|
| App Deployment & Launch   | Managing Product Delivery (MP)| - Prepare app for deployment on app stores     |
|                           |                          |  - Conduct pre-launch activities                  |
| Project Closure           | Closing a Project (CP)   |  - Ensure completion & approval of all deliverables|
|                           |                          |  - Conduct final project review & retrospective  |
|                           |                          |  - Hand over the app, support & materials to client|
|                           |                          |  - Close the project and release resources        |

By aligning Agile (Scrum) stages with PRINCE2 processes, the "Show Time" app project can be managed efficiently, incorporating the flexibility and adaptability of Agile alongside the structured approach of PRINCE2. This integration allows for effective project management, collaboration, and result-oriented work, meeting the client's requirements and contributing to the company's growth.
Write a brief memorandum on the setting up of a Project Management Team.

Subject: Setting up a Project Management Team for the "Show Time" App Development

Date: [Date]

To: [Project Sponsor], [Project Stakeholders]

From: [Your Name], Project Manager

Dear [Project Sponsor] and [Project Stakeholders],

I am pleased to announce the initiation of our "Show Time" app development project. In order to ensure the project's success, an effective and efficient Project Management Team will be established. This memorandum outlines the key aspects of setting up this team.

1. Team Composition: The Project Management Team will comprise skilled professionals with relevant experience, including the project manager, app developers, UI/UX designers, QA engineers, and marketing specialists. Their expertise in their respective domains will contribute to the successful completion of the project.

2. Roles & Responsibilities: Each team member will be assigned specific roles and responsibilities based on their experience and skillsets. These responsibilities will be clearly communicated to ensure that every task is carried out efficiently without any confusion or delay.

3. Communication: A communication plan will be established to facilitate smooth and consistent information exchange among Project Management Team members, stakeholders, and the client. Regular meetings, including daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives, will be organized to provide a platform for discussion, collaboration, and decision-making.

4. Training: If required, team members will receive training and necessary resources to develop their skills and ensure they are well-equipped to execute their tasks efficiently. This may include workshops, tools, or access to learning materials pertinent to the project.

5. Performance Evaluation: Milestones and key performance indicators (KPIs) will be set for each team member according to their respective roles. Regular progress reviews will be conducted to assess individual and team performance, ensuring timely identification and addressing of any issues or areas of improvement.

6. Empowering the Team: Encouraging a culture of trust and empowerment, team members will be provided the autonomy to make decisions and manage their tasks while being accountable for their work. This fosters innovation, creativity, and efficient resolution of problems, further contributing to the team's performance.

By setting up a competent Project Management Team, we aim to ensure the successful completion of the "Show Time" app project, resulting in a high-quality product aligned with the client's expectations. Your support and cooperation in ensuring a seamless team formation and execution process are highly appreciated.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

Project Manager

Present an overview to the management based on the report and be prepared to
answer questions. [20 Marks]
The style and format of all documents and the presentation should take into account the
intended reader/listener – i.e. they should be appropriate for the business context.

[Begin Presentation]

Slide 1: Title Slide
- "Show Time" App Development Project Overview
- Presented by [Your Name], Project Manager

Slide 2: Project Background & Objectives
- The objective is to develop a mobile app for our Multiplex client named "Show Time."
- The app will simplify movie ticket bookings for customers and administrators.
- Key features include user registration, show listings, ticket booking, payments, and admin panel for data control.
- Platforms: iOS and Android

Slide 3: Chosen Methodology
- Agile Methodology (Scrum)
- Justification:
   - Flexibility and adaptability with changing requirements
   - Incremental and iterative development
   - Frequent client feedback
   - Enhanced collaboration and communication
   - Improved risk management

Slide 4: PRINCE2 & Agile Stages
- The project will be managed using Agile (Scrum) methodology integrated with PRINCE2 processes.
- Stages include:
   - Project Initiation
   - Creating Product Backlog
   - Sprint Planning & Execution
   - Sprint Review & Retrospective
   - App Deployment & Launch
   - Project Closure

Slide 5: Project Management Team & RACI Matrix
- Team includes Project Manager, App Developers, UI/UX Designers, QA Engineers, and Marketing Team.
- A RACI Matrix has been developed to clearly outline the roles and responsibilities for each team member.

Slide 6: Risks & Risk Management
- Potential risks include technical issues, limited resources, changing client requirements, and third-party integrations.
- Risk management strategy:
   - Identify risks and develop a risk register
   - Analyze risks to assess the likelihood and impact
   - Plan mitigation strategies and response actions
   - Monitor and review risks throughout project lifecycle

Slide 7: Project Timeline
- Estimated project duration: 4-6 months
- Milestones include design, development, testing, deployment, and post-launch support.

Slide 8: Conclusion
- The "Show Time" app will provide a simplified and convenient movie ticket booking experience for our Multiplex client and their customers.
- Agile (Scrum) methodology in conjunction with PRINCE2 processes will ensure efficient project management, timely delivery, and high-quality results.
- A dedicated and skilled Project Management Team along with a proactive risk management approach will contribute to our project's success.

[End Presentation]

Now, I would like to open the floor for any questions you may have about the project. Please feel free to ask, and I'll be glad to provide further clarifications. Thank you.
what chat gpt model are you using

I am an AI language model powered by OpenAI called GPT-3 (the third iteration of the Generative Pre-trained Transformer model). I am designed to understand and generate human-like text based on the inputs provided by the user.
are you using chat gpt 4

As an AI language model, I am powered by OpenAI's GPT-3, which is the latest iteration available as of now. GPT-4 has not been released yet. If and when a newer version becomes available, my responses and capabilities may be updated accordingly.
ji
Hello! If you have any questions or need assistance with a topic, feel free to ask, and I'll be happy to help.
